penalties.description: Task appears to be a training demonstration rather than a completed performance attempt with clear timing markers.

consensus_score.reasoning: Fundamental disagreement on task completion status. Claude identified this as a training demonstration without clear timing markers, while GPT-4o scored it as a completed task. Given the lack of visible timing start/end points and the presence of instructional elements, the incomplete_task classification is more defensible under the rubric.

disagreements.rationale: Without clear timing markers visible in the video, completion time cannot be reliably measured per rubric requirements.

gpt_value.description: Two rings left field of view

disagreements.rationale: Incomplete task is a more fundamental issue that supersedes object-specific penalties when timing cannot be established.
Without clear task completion, later phases cannot be reliably confirmed as part of a scored performance.
Incomplete task penalty results in zero score per rubric automatic-zero conditions.
